sources.name: sentinel2
sources.notes: Suitable for detecting large vessels; limited for small boats

sources.name: sentinel1
sources.notes: All-weather capability; good for ship wake detection

sources.name: planet
sources.notes: Daily revisit; good resolution for medium vessels

sources.name: maxar
sources.notes: Highest resolution; can identify vessel details and hull numbers

sources.name: skyfi
sources.notes: Aggregator for multiple satellite providers; on-demand tasking

regions_of_interest.name: Guam Approaches
regions_of_interest.notes: Reuters 2026: Chinese research vessels mapped waters surrounding Guam where US nuclear submarines are stationed

regions_of_interest.name: Philippine Sea - East of Philippines
regions_of_interest.notes: Reuters 2026: Most comprehensive Chinese ocean surveying conducted here, along First Island Chain

regions_of_interest.name: Luzon Strait
regions_of_interest.notes: Reuters 2026: China deployed advanced sensors in strait through which US submarines transit to South China Sea

regions_of_interest.name: Malacca Strait Approaches
regions_of_interest.notes: Reuters 2026: Dong Fang Hong 3 criss-crossed waters between Sri Lanka and Indonesia covering Malacca approaches

regions_of_interest.name: Ninety East Ridge - Indian Ocean
regions_of_interest.name_zh: 東經九十度海嶺-印度洋
regions_of_interest.notes: Reuters 2026: Chinese sensor array along underwater mountain range, astride approach to Malacca Strait

regions_of_interest.name: Hawaii Approaches
regions_of_interest.notes: Reuters 2026: Chinese vessels mapped waters around Hawaii, a US regional military hub

regions_of_interest.name: Wake Atoll Approaches
regions_of_interest.notes: Reuters 2026: Surveying near US military facilities on Wake atoll

regions_of_interest.name: Christmas Island - Indian Ocean
regions_of_interest.notes: Reuters 2026: Chinese vessels scouted around Christmas Island, on route between SCS and Australian submarine base

regions_of_interest.name: Arctic - West and North of Alaska
regions_of_interest.notes: Reuters 2026: Chinese vessels mapped seabed west and north of Alaska, essential sea route into Arctic

ais_sources.name: MarineTraffic
ais_sources.notes: CCG vessels often disable AIS; use as supplementary

ais_sources.name: Starboard Maritime Intelligence
ais_sources.notes: Ship-tracking platform used by Reuters to track 42 Chinese research vessels across 5+ years. Key source for research vessel movement data.

tracked_research_vessels.operator: Ocean University of China (中國海洋大學)
known_activities: Oct 2024: Checked Chinese ocean sensors near Japan capable of identifying undersea objects
Mar 2025: Criss-crossed waters between Sri Lanka and Indonesia (Malacca Strait approaches)
Confirmed deep-sea mapping per co-authored scientific papers

tracked_research_vessels.operator: Ministry of Natural Resources (自然資源部)
Chinese Academy of Sciences - Institute of Oceanology (中國科學院海洋研究所)

transparent_ocean_program.founder: Wu Lixin (吳立新), Ocean University of China
transparent_ocean_program.oversight: Qingdao National Laboratory for Marine Science and Technology (青島海洋科學與技術國家實驗室)
military_partners: China Naval Submarine Academy (中國海軍潛艦學院)
coverage: South China Sea (deep-sea basin observation system)
transparent_ocean_program.capabilities: Real-time data on water temperature, salinity, currents, subsea movements

us_intelligence_assessment.source: Rear Admiral Mike Brookes, Director, US Office of Naval Intelligence
us_intelligence_assessment.testimony: China has dramatically expanded surveying efforts providing data that enables submarine navigation, concealment, and positioning of seabed sensors or weapons. Potential military intelligence collection by Chinese research vessels represents a strategic concern.

key_findings: At least 8 vessels confirmed conducting seabed mapping
Focused on First Island Chain, Guam, Hawaii, Indian Ocean, Arctic
China building detailed knowledge of marine conditions for submarine warfare
Civil-military fusion: civilian research directly supports PLA Navy
